members.name: Depth
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_DEPTH], the depth of the extrusion.

members.name: Material
members.summary: Material to use for the resulting mesh. The UV maps the top half of the material to the extruded shape (U along the length of the extrusions and V around the outline of the [member polygon]), the bottom-left quarter to the front end face, and the bottom-right quarter to the back end face.

members.name: Mode
members.summary: The [member mode] used to extrude the [member polygon].

members.name: PathContinuousU
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], by default, the top half of the [member material] is stretched along the entire length of the extruded shape. If [code]false[/code] the top half of the material is repeated every step of the extrusion.

members.name: PathInterval
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], the path interval or ratio of path points to extrusions.

members.name: PathIntervalType
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], this will determine if the interval should be by distance ([constant PATH_INTERVAL_DISTANCE]) or subdivision fractions ([constant PATH_INTERVAL_SUBDIVIDE]).

members.name: PathJoined
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], if [code]true[/code] the ends of the path are joined, by adding an extrusion between the last and first points of the path.

members.name: PathLocal
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], if [code]true[/code] the [Transform3D] of the [CSGPolygon3D] is used as the starting point for the extrusions, not the [Transform3D] of the [member path_node].

members.name: PathNode
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], the location of the [Path3D] object used to extrude the [member polygon].

members.name: PathRotation
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], the path rotation method used to rotate the [member polygon] as it is extruded.

members.name: PathRotationAccurate
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], if [code]true[/code] the polygon will be rotated according to the proper tangent of the path at the sampled points. If [code]false[/code] an approximation is used, which decreases in accuracy as the number of subdivisions decreases.

members.name: PathSimplifyAngle
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], extrusions that are less than this angle, will be merged together to reduce polygon count.

members.name: PathUDistance
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_PATH], this is the distance along the path, in meters, the texture coordinates will tile. When set to 0, texture coordinates will match geometry exactly with no tiling.

members.name: Polygon
members.summary: The point array that defines the 2D polygon that is extruded. This can be a convex or concave polygon with 3 or more points. The polygon must [i]not[/i] have any intersecting edges. Otherwise, triangulation will fail and no mesh will be generated. [b]Note:[/b] If only 1 or 2 points are defined in [member polygon], no mesh will be generated.

members.name: SmoothFaces
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], applies smooth shading to the extrusions.

members.name: SpinDegrees
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_SPIN], the total number of degrees the [member polygon] is rotated when extruding.

members.name: SpinSides
members.summary: When [member mode] is [constant MODE_SPIN], the number of extrusions made.

values.name: MODE_DEPTH
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded along the negative Z axis.

values.name: MODE_PATH
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded along the [Path3D] specified in [member path_node].

values.name: MODE_SPIN
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded by rotating it around the Y axis.

values.name: PATH_INTERVAL_DISTANCE
values.summary: When [member mode] is set to [constant MODE_PATH], [member path_interval] will determine the distance, in meters, each interval of the path will extrude.

values.name: PATH_INTERVAL_SUBDIVIDE
values.summary: When [member mode] is set to [constant MODE_PATH], [member path_interval] will subdivide the polygons along the path.

values.name: PATH_ROTATION_PATH
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape is rotated along the path, but it is not rotated around the path axis. [b]Note:[/b] Requires the path Z coordinates to continually decrease to ensure viable shapes.

values.name: PATH_ROTATION_PATH_FOLLOW
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape follows the path and its rotations around the path axis.

values.name: PATH_ROTATION_POLYGON
values.summary: The [member polygon] shape is not rotated. [b]Note:[/b] Requires the path Z coordinates to continually decrease to ensure viable shapes.

constants.name: MODE_DEPTH
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded along the negative Z axis.

constants.name: MODE_SPIN
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded by rotating it around the Y axis.

constants.name: MODE_PATH
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape is extruded along the [Path3D] specified in [member path_node].

constants.name: PATH_ROTATION_POLYGON
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape is not rotated. [b]Note:[/b] Requires the path Z coordinates to continually decrease to ensure viable shapes.

constants.name: PATH_ROTATION_PATH
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape is rotated along the path, but it is not rotated around the path axis. [b]Note:[/b] Requires the path Z coordinates to continually decrease to ensure viable shapes.

constants.name: PATH_ROTATION_PATH_FOLLOW
constants.summary: The [member polygon] shape follows the path and its rotations around the path axis.

constants.name: PATH_INTERVAL_DISTANCE
constants.summary: When [member mode] is set to [constant MODE_PATH], [member path_interval] will determine the distance, in meters, each interval of the path will extrude.

constants.name: PATH_INTERVAL_SUBDIVIDE
constants.summary: When [member mode] is set to [constant MODE_PATH], [member path_interval] will subdivide the polygons along the path.
